task_completion.visual_evidence: An image editing application (like GIMP, Photoshop, or a default system image editor) is open on the screen.

task_completion.description: Load the target image into the editor.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The target image (a photograph, likely a landscape or outdoor scene from Unsplash) is visible within the workspace of the image editor.

task_completion.description: Access the levels, contrast, or color adjustment menu.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A dropdown menu or dialog box for 'Levels', 'Colors', 'Adjustments', or 'Auto' enhancements is open and visible on screen.

The 'Auto' button within a Levels dialog is clicked, or an 'Auto Levels'/'Stretch Contrast' option is selected. The image should visibly change, showing improved contrast (darker shadows, brighter highlights).

task_completion.description: Save or export the enhanced image.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save', 'Save As', or 'Export' dialog is open, or there is a notification/visual confirmation that the modified image has been saved.

integrity.description: The agent used the graphical user interface to edit the image.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent clicking through menus and dialogs in an image editor, rather than using terminal commands (like ImageMagick's 'convert') to process the image.

integrity.description: The agent did not use a Python script to bypass the image editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: There are no terminal windows open showing Python scripts (e.g., using PIL or OpenCV) being executed to adjust the image levels.

integrity.description: The image was genuinely modified within the application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The visual change in the image's contrast/exposure happens within the image editor's canvas, rather than the agent simply downloading a pre-edited image or replacing the file directly in the file manager.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target image is an Unsplash photo with dimensions 800x533 or 800x477. The task requires applying an 'auto levels' or equivalent contrast/exposure enhancement. A successful enhancement will visibly increase contrast (by at least 15%) and stretch the histogram, changing the intensity of >10% of the pixels by more than 20 units. Look for visual confirmation that the image's darks became darker and lights became lighter in the editor canvas.
